author: kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa kavirāja gosvāmī
verses:
kuṅkumākta⋅kāñcanābja⋅garva⋅hāri⋅gaurabhā
pītanāñcitābja⋅gandha⋅kīrti⋅nindi⋅saurabhā
vallaveśa⋅sūnu⋅sarva⋅vāñchitārtha⋅sādhikā
mahyamastu pāda⋅padma⋅dāsyadāstu rādhikā

kauravinda⋅kānti⋅nindi⋅citra⋅paṭṭa⋅śāṭikā
kṛṣṇa⋅matta⋅bhṛṅga⋅keli⋅phulla⋅puṣpa⋅vāṭikā
kṛṣṇa⋅nitya⋅saṅgamārtha⋅padma⋅bandhu⋅rādhikā
mahyamastu pāda⋅padma⋅dāsyadāstu rādhikā

saukumārya⋅sṛṣṭa⋅pallavāli⋅kīrti⋅nigrahā
candra⋅candanotpalendu⋅sevya⋅śīta⋅vigrahā
svābhimarṣa⋅vallavīśa⋅kāma⋅tāpādhikā
mahyamastu pāda⋅padma⋅dāsyadāstu rādhikā

viśvavandya⋅yauvatābhivanditāpi jā ramā
rūpa⋅navya⋅yauvanādi⋅sampadā na jatsamā
śīla⋅hārda⋅līlayā ca sā jato‛sti nādhikā
mahyamastu pāda⋅padma⋅dāsyadāstu rādhikā

rāsa⋅lāsya⋅gīta⋅narma⋅satkalāli⋅paṇḍitā
prema⋅ramya⋅rūpa⋅veśa⋅sadguṇāli⋅maṇḍitā
viśva⋅navya⋅gopa⋅yoṣidālito‛pi jādhikā
mahyamastu pāda⋅padma⋅dāsyadāstu rādhikā

nitya⋅nava⋅rūpa⋅keli⋅kṛṣṇa⋅bhāva⋅sampadā
kṛṣṇa⋅rāga⋅bandha⋅gopa⋅yauvateṣu kampadā
kṛṣṇa⋅rūpa⋅veśa⋅keli⋅lagna⋅satsamādhikā
mahyamastu pāda⋅padma⋅dāsyadāstu rādhikā

sveda⋅kampa⋅kaṇṭakāśru⋅gadgadādi⋅sañcitā⋅
marṣa⋅harṣa⋅vāmatādi⋅bhāva⋅bhūṣaṇāñcitā
kṛṣṇa⋅netra⋅toṣi⋅ratna⋅maṇḍanāli⋅dādhikā
mahyamastu pāda⋅padma⋅dāsyadāstu rādhikā

jā kṣaṇārdha⋅kṛṣṇa⋅viprayoga⋅santatoditā⋅
neka⋅dainya⋅cāpalādi⋅bhāva⋅vṛnda⋅moditā
jatnalabdha⋅kṛṣṇa⋅saṅga⋅nirgatākhilādhikā
mahyamastu pāda⋅padma⋅dāsyadāstu rādhikā

aṣṭakena jastvetena nauti kṛṣṇa⋅vallabhāṁ
darśane‛pi śailajādi⋅yoṣidāli⋅durlabhām
kṛṣṇa⋅saṅga⋅nanditātma⋅dāsya⋅sīdhu⋅bhājanaṁ
taṁ karoti nanditāli⋅sañcayāśu sā janam
en_translation:
Anointed with vermilion, She whose golden lotus-like beauty defeats all pride. She whose fragrance, surpassing the scent of a blossoming lotus, proclaims Her fame. She who fulfills all the desires of the son of the king of the cowherds. May that Rādhā, the beloved of Kṛṣṇa, grant me service to Her lotus feet.

She whose colorful silken garments defeat the luster of sapphire. She is a garden of blossoming flowers for Kṛṣṇa, the maddened bee, to sport in. Rādhā, who is like the sun for the eternal union with Kṛṣṇa. May She grant me service to Her lotus feet.

Her tenderness surpasses the beauty of young sprouts. She whose cool form is served by the moon, sandalwood, lotus, and camphor. She who relieves the fever of the Lord of the cowherds by Her touch. May She grant me service to Her lotus feet.

Even the goddess of fortune, who is worshiped by all the worlds, is not equal to Her in the wealth of beauty, youth, and other qualities. In character, affection, and pastimes, She is supreme. May She grant me service to Her lotus feet.

She is skilled in all the excellent arts of the rāsa dance, singing, and joking. Adorned with the charming beauty of love, attire, and all good qualities. She who is superior even to all the young gopīs of the world. May She grant me service to Her lotus feet.

Always endowed with the wealth of ever-new beauty, sports, and Kṛṣṇa's moods, She makes the young gopīs tremble in their bond of love for Him. She is perfectly absorbed in the form, attire, and pastimes of Kṛṣṇa. May She grant me service to Her lotus feet.

Filled with emotions like perspiration, trembling, thrills of ecstasy, and a choked voice, She is adorned with the ornaments of various moods like indignation, joy, and charm. She bestows a multitude of jeweled ornaments that please Kṛṣṇa's eyes. May She grant me service to Her lotus feet.

She who is pleased by the multitude of emotions like extreme humility and restlessness, which arise from even a half-moment's separation from Kṛṣṇa. She who becomes entirely perfect upon obtaining Kṛṣṇa's company. May She grant me service to Her lotus feet.

He who praises the beloved of Kṛṣṇa with these eight verses—She who is difficult for even the greatest goddesses to behold—is quickly made a recipient of the nectar of service to Her feet by Her, making all Her companions joyful.
